description: Phase 2D — Interactive artifacts: quiz, flashcards, app scaffolder. Heaviest LLM creativity of any phase. Reuses /generate router.

userStories.title: Implement generate-quiz handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/generate quiz <topic>` to produce a standalone HTML quiz (multiple choice + short answer) I can open locally to self-test.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate-quiz/SKILL.md
LLM generates a quiz JSON schema (question, type: mcq|short, options?, correct, explanation, source-page) from topic wiki pages
Ships a static HTML + vanilla JS template at .claude/skills/generate-quiz/templates/quiz.html that renders the JSON
Output: vaults/<vault>/artifacts/quiz/<topic>-<date>.html (self-contained) + .meta.yaml + .questions.json for re-ingest support
Supports --count <n> (default 10) and --difficulty easy|medium|hard
Each question links back to its source wiki page (critical for close-the-loop fidelity testing)
userStories.notes: Self-contained HTML means no server. Good for emailing to a study partner. — Done: generate-quiz handler with self-contained HTML template (Observatory palette, vanilla JS, no deps). Questions schema supports MCQ + short-answer with per-question `source` field for close-the-loop testing. --count (default 10), --difficulty easy|medium|hard. .questions.json kept alongside .html as re-renderable source. LocalStorage persistence intentionally omitted (one-liner if asked).

userStories.title: Implement generate-flashcards handler
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/generate flashcards <topic>` to produce an Anki .apkg file so I can import into Anki for spaced-repetition study.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate-flashcards/SKILL.md
Uses the genanki Python library; lazy-installs via pip/pipx
LLM generates card pairs (front=question/concept, back=answer/definition, source=wiki page path)
Output: vaults/<vault>/artifacts/flashcards/<topic>-<date>.apkg + .meta.yaml + .cards.csv for re-ingest support
Deck name derived from topic; tags include the vault name and topic
Cards include a 'source' field at the bottom linking to the wiki page
Documents how to import into Anki desktop/mobile
userStories.notes: CSV sidecar is what close-the-loop testing will re-ingest, since Anki apkg is a SQLite file and awkward to parse. — Done: generate-flashcards handler with genanki lazy-install via pipx (fallback pip --user), stable sha256-derived deck_id + model_id so Anki re-imports update cards in-place (no duplicate-deck pain), hierarchical deck naming `llm-wiki::<vault>::<topic>` (plays well with Anki's folder view), 4-column CSV (front/back/source/tags) with RFC 4180 quoting kept alongside .apkg as re-renderable source. Desktop + mobile (AnkiMobile/AnkiDroid) import walkthrough documented. Cloze cards and media support deferred — basic front/back only for 2D.

userStories.title: Implement generate-app handler (scaffolder)
userStories.description: As a user, I want `/generate app <topic>` to scaffold an interactive explorable web app (e.g., comparison explorer, timeline viewer) populated with data from wiki pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/generate-app/SKILL.md
Ships 2 app templates in .claude/skills/generate-app/templates/: 'comparison-explorer' (for comparison-type wiki pages) and 'timeline' (for date-indexed wiki content)
LLM reads topic wiki pages, selects the best-fit template, extracts structured data into a JSON fixture, scaffolds a Vite + React or Astro app
Output: vaults/<vault>/artifacts/app/<topic>-<date>/ (directory with README, package.json, src/, plus .meta.yaml)
App is runnable via `cd <app-dir> && pnpm install && pnpm dev` — documented in the generated README
LLM produces a working app, not a stub — verify by running pnpm dev and taking a screenshot
userStories.notes: This is the most ambitious story in 2D. If the LLM-authored components are brittle, ship 'comparison-explorer' only and push timeline to 2D.2. — Done: both templates shipped (comparison-explorer + timeline). Each is a minimal Vite + React + TS app (~200 LOC across package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig, index.html, main.tsx, App.tsx, index.css, data.json, data.schema.json, TEMPLATE.md). Observatory palette, vanilla CSS, no frameworks. Scaffolder logic is `cp -r template/ → app-dir/` + write `data.json` + sed package.json name. LLM auto-picks template or user forces via --template. Data-driven: the only LLM-authored output that varies per generation is src/data.json — template code is static. Template authoring guide in SKILL.md shows it's literally `cp -r` so any Vite project can become a template.

userStories.title: Document Phase 2D in sites/docs
userStories.description: As a user, I want docs for generate-quiz, generate-flashcards, and generate-app including template authoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate-quiz.md with example quiz screenshot
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate-flashcards.md with Anki import walkthrough
Add sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/generate-app.md with template gallery and custom-template authoring guide
Update commands.md reference
Mermaid flow diagrams on each page
userStories.notes: Done: three new Starlight pages — features/generate-quiz.md, features/generate-flashcards.md, features/generate-app.md — each with a Mermaid flow diagram in Observatory palette, usage + flag tables, example output, template authoring notes, troubleshooting table, known limitations, and See Also. features/generate.md handler table + See Also updated to mark 2D handlers ✅. reference/commands.md /generate handler table + internal-skills table updated. generate-app doc includes a full template-authoring guide (custom template workflow + vault-local override + palette + single-file-component guidelines).
